Class Heap

  • java.lang.Object
    • Heap
  • public class Heap
extends java.lang.Object
    Binary heap that stores integer values. It uses ArrayList for storing data.
    See Also:
    add(int), extractMin(), toArray()

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      Heap()
      Creates binary Heap Started size is 0 ArrayList is used as data structure
      Heap​(int[] arr)
      Creates heap from arr elements.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void add​(int val)
      Adds element into heap.
      int extractMin()
      Erase min value from heap with changing of element order.
      int get​(int index)
      Return elements by index.
      int getMin()
      Return minimal element from heap.
      static int[] heapSort​(int[] arr)
      Sorts array using heap construction and deconstruction with extracting min value.
      boolean isEmpty()
      Check heap for empty.
      int size()
      Return size of heap.
      int[] toArray()
      Creates sorted integer array with deconstruction the heap.
      java.lang.String toString()  

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Heap

        public Heap()
        Creates binary Heap Started size is 0 ArrayList is used as data structure
        See Also:
        Heap(int[])

      • Heap

        public Heap​(int[] arr)
        Creates heap from arr elements.
        Parameters:
        arr - is the initial array, from which objects are added to heap.
        See Also:
        siftDown(int)

    • Method Detail

      • add

        public void add​(int val)
        Adds element into heap.
        Parameters:
        val - the int element that will be added in heap.
        See Also:
        siftUp(int)

      • getMin

        public int getMin()
        Return minimal element from heap.
        Returns:
        Minimal element (minimal integer value) from heap.

      • extractMin

        public int extractMin()
        Erase min value from heap with changing of element order.
        Returns:
        the minimal value
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no element in heap.

      • size

        public int size()
        Return size of heap.
        Returns:
        the size of heap.
        See Also:
        toArray()

      • isEmpty

        public boolean isEmpty()
        Check heap for empty.
        Returns:
        true if this list contains at least one element.
        See Also:
        sizeOfHeap

      • toArray

        public int[] toArray()
        Creates sorted integer array with deconstruction the heap. After this operation heap is empty.
        Returns:
        sorted intArray from heap.

      • heapSort

        public static int[] heapSort​(int[] arr)
        Sorts array using heap construction and deconstruction with extracting min value.
        Parameters:
        arr - is the array from which the sorted array will be created.
        Returns:
        sorted array.
        See Also:
        toArray(), Heap(int[])

      • get

        public int get​(int index)
        Return elements by index.
        Parameters:
        index - the index of element that will be returned.
        Returns:
        the element by index.
        Throws:
        java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is invalid.

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
